more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Mutapa’s New Prize: The Secret Plan Behind ZISCOSTEEL’s Sudden Move to the Sovereign Wealth Fund
more.description: The legendary Zimbabwe Iron and Steel Company (ZISCOSTEEL), once the undisputed pride of African industry, has been quietly moved under the umbrella of the Mutapa Investment Fund. But what does this sudden transfer mean for the thousands of former workers left destitute, and for the nation’s industrial future? This investigation delves into the “secret plan” [...] The post Mutapa’s New Prize: The Secret Plan Behind ZISCOSTEEL’s Sudden Move to the Sovereign Wealth Fund first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .

For the dedicated nurses currently engaged in flash demonstrations across Harare, this promise resonates less as a tangible [...] The post The April Salary Mirage: Why Civil Servants’ 2026 Pay Rise Might Be Swallowed by Inflation Before It Arrives first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .

more.title: Zimbabwe’s ban on raw minerals exports puts Chinese firms under pressure
more.description: THE Chinese Embassy in Zimbabwe has warned its nationals and companies operating in the country to exercise caution and strengthen compliance measures following government policy shifts that include a ban on the export of raw minerals and lithium concentrates. Zimbabwe is tightening control over its vast mineral resources, particularly lithium, which has attracted significant Chinese [...] The post Zimbabwe’s ban on raw minerals exports puts Chinese firms under pressure appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Senator riled over fuel price hikes by ZERA, says Zimbabwe blends fuel with ethanol hence prices must be affordable
more.description: A MEMBER of the Senate has blasted authorities for increasing fuel prices twice in less than two weeks. Senator Jerry Gotora of Hwedza, Mashonaland East Province on a point of national interest queried why the Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority (ZERA) continues to hike the precious commodity yet the country has several storage facilities to store [...] The post Senator riled over fuel price hikes by ZERA, says Zimbabwe blends fuel with ethanol hence prices must be affordable appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Opposition Leader Tendai Biti Arrested in Mutare
more.description: MUTARE, Zimbabwe — Prominent opposition politician Tendai Biti was arrested in Mutare on Saturday afternoon, according to local reports. Biti, who is also the convener of the Constitutional Defenders Forum, was reportedly detained while attending a private meeting in the city’s central business district. The meeting is alleged to have been focused on opposition to [...] The post Opposition Leader Tendai Biti Arrested in Mutare appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Teddy Riley’s New Book Is Remarkable, But The Michael Jackson Chapter Will Shock Fans
more.description: Teddy Riley is not just a hitmaker. The Harlem-born icon is one of the architects of modern Black music. Credited with creating New Jack Swing, Riley fused Hip-Hop’s attitude with R&B melody and pop precision. He defined the sound of the late 1980s and 1990s through work with Guy, Blackstreet, Wreckx-n-Effect, Bobby Brown, Keith Sweat, Big Daddy [...] The post Teddy Riley’s New Book Is Remarkable, But The Michael Jackson Chapter Will Shock Fans appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: ‘Market manipulation’: Iran denies US claims it has ‘surplus oil’ stored at sea
more.description: Saman Ghodousi, spokesperson for the Iranian Ministry of Petroleum, dismissed US claims that Iranian crude is stored at sea and ready for release and rejected remarks by US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent that Washington would “ease restrictions to boost supply.” “Iran currently has no crude oil left on the water and no surplus intended for [...] The post ‘Market manipulation’: Iran denies US claims it has ‘surplus oil’ stored at sea appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .
